title: Some Useful Discrete-Time Signal Models
summary: This page presents Figure 3.9 with examples of discrete-time exponentials, illustrating decaying, alternating-sign decaying, faster decaying, and growing sequences. The text explains how the magnitude and sign of γ determine whether γ^n decays, grows, or alternates, and Drill 3.7 asks students to sketch several DT exponentials and relate them to the location of γ in the complex plane.
